Package org.solenopsis.soap.metadata

Class DelegateGroup

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.Metadata
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.DelegateGroup
public class DelegateGroup extends Metadata

Java class for DelegateGroup complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="DelegateGroup">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}Metadata">
       <sequence>
         <element name="customObjects" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="groups" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="label" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="loginAccess" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean"/>
         <element name="permissionSetGroups" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="permissionSets" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="profiles" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="roles" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

  • Method Details

    • getCustomObjects

      public List<String> getCustomObjects()
      Gets the value of the customObjects property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the customObjects property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getCustomObjects().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list String

      Returns:
      The value of the customObjects property.

    • getGroups

      public List<String> getGroups()
      Gets the value of the groups property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the groups property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getGroups().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list String

      Returns:
      The value of the groups property.

      public List<String> getPermissionSetGroups()
      Gets the value of the permissionSetGroups property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the permissionSetGroups property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getPermissionSetGroups().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list String

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the profiles property.
